tokens.text: Ister
Dispirited — literally. Now, he wanders the land, searching for his wayward soul.

“Ister” is not a name, but a fragment of one. For he himself is not a person, but a fragment of a person.
A powerful mage once sought to rid himself of his body, aiming to free the spirit. The ritual succeeded — the spirit was freed. However, the body did not perish, instead rising to walk. Now, Ister wanders the world aimlessly, seeking the second half of his name... The second half of himself.
Wayfarer
He has +{0}% Movement points for every {1} hero level(s). Moves significantly more efficiently on roads.
Leon Sticky‑Fingers
He knew the land like his five (sticky) fingers — yet they still got him on a leash.

Leon was once the most elusive bandit, outsmarting officials and knights alike. In the end, it was the inquisition that finally caught him. Rather than be summarily executed, he was offered a deal: serve the Church, using his unmatched skills to navigate even the densest forests, and they would spare his life.
Devout souls fear not death, but Leon is far from devout. So he agreed to wear the Church collar, a magical shackle that binds him. Perhaps one day it will break.
Pathfinder
His movement takes no terrain penalties. He also has +{0} Sight Radius, plus another +{1} for every {2} hero level(s).
John Johnson
Just a soldier.

John was just a soldier until the Ravenshore campaign, where he carried his fallen commander to safety and led a retreat. Proving himself in battle, he rose through the ranks as a general who fought alongside his men.
The loyalty of his army never wavered — even against a dragon. When they slew the beast together, John was the last to believe it. He still sees himself as just a soldier, but hero or not, his greatest deeds may yet lie ahead.
Salt of the Earth

Swordsman growth in your cities increases by {0}.
Under his command, Swordsmen gain {1} Speed, {1} Initiative, and {2}% HP. Their Attack and Defense increase by {3} for every {4} hero levels, and enemy Swordsmen lose an equal amount of Attack and Defense.
Kestrel
Whatever you’re doing to impress her, save both of you the trouble, a’ight?

She was once a bandit, Leon’s partner in crime.
One day, the pair stopped in a village in the path of Kel’Ghul. The villagers were defenseless against the coming invasion, having never learned how to fight. So Kestrel taught them.
Leon saw this as a betrayal of the pair’s freedom. But maybe the only way to protect freedom is to give up a little of it yourself.
Jaeger

Crossbowman growth in your cities increases by {0}.
Under her command, Crossbowmen gain {1} Speed, {1} Initiative, and {2}% HP. Their Attack and Defense increase by {3} for every {4} hero levels, and enemy Crossbowmen lose an equal amount of Attack and Defense.
Aeos the Exalted
The sun kissed her cheek; every act of hers is blessed, whether you believe it or not.

When the Hive attacked Aeos’s village and burned her face, a beam of light struck from the heavens. The village was saved, but the light took away Aeos’s ordinary human vision and the gift of speech. Yet she saw the Truth: that the Sun had chosen her for a sacred mission.
The Church approved of this truth, making Aeos an official saint. Any mission of hers is blessed. Never you doubt that.
Rousing Presence
Friendly creatures have +{0} Morale. They also have a higher chance of triggering the positive Morale effect: +{1}% chance per Morale point. For every {3} levels, this bonus increases by {2}%.
Heretic Avis
The Philosopher’s Stone will bring it all back — his face, his wife, his life. It must.

Avis used to be a renowned knight, with a wondrous life of nobility and marriage awaiting him. All of it was lost in a horrendous fire that left him disfigured and his fiancée dead. After receiving no answers from the Church, he donned a mask to hide his scars and seek out the Philosopher’s Stone, a miraculous item that can restore health and youth.
No matter how hard he searches for it, the Stone eludes Avis. Maybe it’s just a myth — but it’s not like he has any other choice.
Gem Seeker
He produces +{0} Gem per day, plus another +{1} for every {2} hero level(s). Increases the amount of Gems found on the map by {3}%.
Keandra
Think fast. Ride faster.

Keandra was always quick and clever. But during an expedition, she encountered Marl. When the ugly creature’s gaze fell on her, the ground turned to sand, wax, web — liquid beneath her feet. And her legs failed her.
In that moment, she realized speed and bravery weren’t enough — she needed strength, better tools, and sturdier legs. So she became a cavalier, a jouster. And she swore never to feel helpless again.
Jouster

Cavalry growth in your cities increases by {0}.
Under her command, Cavalry gain {1} Speed, {1} Initiative, and {2}% HP. Their Attack and Defense increase by {3} for every {4} hero levels, and enemy Cavalry lose an equal amount of Attack and Defense.
Lord Edgar
The Church of Sun teaches that perfection is earned — those who fall short simply did not try hard enough.

Glory is everything to Lord Edgar. Years of training with the finest masters have made him a brilliant warrior, but on the battlefield (and perhaps beyond it) he cares only for himself. His sword strikes are near flawless, his skill undeniable.
To him, everyone else is just the backdrop — and one day he might pay for his oversight.
True Lord
Friendly creatures gain {0}% of his Attack and Defense (as Attack and Defense), plus another {1}% for every {2} hero level(s).
Old Lord Mandall
A warrior learns from the past — both from triumphs and defeats.

Lord Mandall has always been a warrior with the mindset of an archivist. While his comrades and competitors eagerly sought new battles, he found it more interesting to sit in the library, studying the lessons to be gleaned from conflicts long since unfolded. There’s little point in rushing into battle oneself, he reasoned; let it come to you instead.
Some say he awaits death in the same way, like one final enemy ever approaching. So far it has eluded him, and he is still waiting — after more than a hundred years.
Heroic Smite
His Heroic Strike deals +{0} Damage, plus another +{1} for every {2} hero level(s). It also increases all Damage taken by the target by {3}% until the end of the next round. This effect is stackable.
Merry Elias
What is a perfect man? Is the bliss after the fifth jug not blessedly perfect?

What is self‑improvement, if not the pursuit of perfect happiness — best found at the bottom of a glass, or better yet, five?
Sometimes, it seems Elias joined the Church just for its comforts. Yet fate has gifted him a body that endures endless indulgence, and a spirit that only grows stronger with each drink. The more he drinks, the more potent his magic becomes.
Truly, libations reveal the depths of the soul!
Spiritual Vigor
He can use the Spellbook one more time per battle round. His maximum mana increases by {1}% for every {2} hero level(s).
He can use the Spellbook one additional time per battle round. Spells cost +{0}% mana after each use. Maximum mana increases by {1}% for every {2} hero level(s).
He can use each Global Map spell +{3} time(s) per day. His maximum mana increases by {0}%, plus another {1}% for every {2} hero level(s).
Pip
A nameless village boy had one chance to learn to read — now the world is an open book.

After Lord Edgar’s entourage fell to a bandit ambush, Pip and his mother — a pair of simple peasants — helped him recover. Conceited as he was, Edgar then valued hard work he witness and took on the sharp‑witted boy as a servant.
In this new and unfamiliar environment, Pip quickly realized the power of reading and writing. Now, the world is an open book to him, and he still remembers how dim life was before he stepped into the wonders of a library.
Wish to Learn
When leveling up, he gains {3} additional attribute point(s) for every {4} hero level(s). He gains +{1}% XP for every {2} hero level(s).
Zenith
Climb the ranks; rise to the top — only then will you shine.

Perhaps Zenith’s path would have been different if not for the peculiarities of her twin brother, Nadir. To protect him, Zenith needed influence — so she rose to become a leader of the Church.
Sometimes, striving for the best version of oneself means embracing light… while also holding onto a trace of darkness.
Weaving the Light

Lightweaver growth in your cities increases by {0}.
Under her command, Lightweavers gain {1} Speed, {1} Initiative, and {2}% HP. Their Attack and Defense increase by {3} for every {4} hero levels, and enemy Lightweavers lose an equal amount of Attack and Defense.
Lia the Untethered One
A human soul is vast — rise in the Church, and shed the body’s chains.

Lia was so wholly devoted to self‑perfection that her face became replaced by a vague, radiant form. Her soul had broken free, purified of body and shape. Unlike others who have reached this state, Lia does not hide behind a mask. She believes people turn away not from fear, but because her brilliance is blinding.
Her ascension has granted her unparalleled mastery over Daylight Magic — after all, it’s hard to fight what you can barely look at.
Radiating Knowledge

She receives +{0} level to all Daylight spells, plus another +{1} for every {2} hero levels. Casting Daylight spells does not prevent her from using other spells of the same Magic School. Enemy hero cannot cast Daylight spells.
Julius
Kindness is the rarest treasure, yet the most abundant gift.

Julius, born noble and Church‑raised, saw clergy hypocrisy firsthand. How can one claim to train the soul while the body still aches? With an exceptional healing talent, he left it all behind, staff in hand, to aid common folk. He mended broken bones, assisted births, and cured livestock — perhaps helping more souls than any grand ascension.
Compassionate Healer

He starts with the “Masterful Healing Water” spell. This version of the spell dispels all negative effects from friendly creatures.
While casting this spell, effective Spell Power is increased by {0} for every {1} hero level(s).
Vesper
Who is he, truly? And whose blessing does he bear?

When Vesper arrived in Jadame, he joined the Church of the Sun, claiming to be a mage from the obscure Order of Lightwood. He rose to prominence by bestowing powerful blessings and enchantments, yet the inquisition remained wary of his motives. To test him, they sent him to the frontlines in the Murmurwoods, unaware that this assignment played right into his own designs.
While under constant scrutiny, Vesper bides his time, pursuing hidden goals while maintaining his carefully crafted facade of a humble mage.
Blessing

He starts with the “Masterful Blessing” spell. This version of the spell makes the target always deal maximum Damage. 
While casting this spell, effective Spell Power is increased by {0} for every {1} hero level(s).
Anastasia the Meek
Born with a stutter, she thought herself unworthy. But the Church of Sun has taught her that hymns carry over simple speech.

Anastasia’s stutter meant she never took the inquisitor’s exam. But while tending to her duties in the Church, she realized that when she sang, her voice was steady — and her songs had a certain effect on those who listened.
She prefers to see the best in people and believes in their strength. And perhaps, when she sings her favorite hymn to Arina, others feel it too.
Hymn to Arina

She starts with the “Masterful Arina’s Touch” spell. This version of the spell increases target’s Speed by {2}. 
While casting this spell, effective Spell Power is increased by {0} for every {1} hero level(s).
Nadir
No faith is bad faith if it serves the Church of Sun.

Since childhood, Nadir had a knack for spotting weaknesses in others and using them to his advantage. His skills could have risen him high in the Church of Sun, but Nadir had little interest in its ideals. He could have pursued magic elsewhere, but his sister, Zenith, worked hard to keep him within the Church’s protection.
Out of gratitude, he stays by her side. And, in a way, he is her greatest weakness.
Heart of Hearts

He starts with the “Masterful Vulnerability” spell. This version of the spell makes the target always take maximum Damage. 
While casting this spell, effective Spell Power is increased by {0} for every {1} hero level(s).
Clarissa
Warriors and mages are scary, but nothing tops an enraged tax collector.

Clarissa is neither a warrior nor a mage. She is something far more formidable: an official. Well‑educated in magic, religion, and military affairs, her primary concern is ensuring everything follows the proper procedures. Strictly. As it should be.
Yet somehow, the revenue she deposits into the city coffers always surpasses that of her equally “honest” peers. Perhaps she really is blessed.
Wealthy
She produces +{0} Gold per day, plus another +{1} for every {2} hero level(s). Increases the amount of Gold found on the map by {3}%.
Bulwark
A literal bastion, forged from stone and human ashes, brought to unlife to defend.

Bulwark was created through an ancient, forbidden ritual — melting the town’s walls together with its human defenders to withstand a minotaur raid. A grim response to brutal times.
Tireless and single‑minded, it doesn’t quite remember what it protects — only that it must. Its unwavering nature and imposing presence make it an ideal leader for necromantic armies.
Built Different

Friendly creatures take –{0}% Damage from basic attacks, and an additional –{1}% for every {2} hero level(s). For every {4} hero levels, creatures in his army take –{3}% Damage from Ranged and Long Reach attacks.
King‑of‑Kings
A walking amalgamation of countless undead memories, bound into a single form.

As his mind began failing through the repetition of necromantic rituals, this lich gradually formed a narcissistic personality.
Calling itself the King‑of‑Kings, it drew followers who wished to be remembered, to honor the dead, or to learn from the past. Eventually, its unbounded growth caught the attention of the Necromancers Guild — who recognized its potential and made it an agent.
Charismatic
It keeps its army when fleeing from battle against neutral squads. +{0}% Persuasion Power when using Diplomacy, plus another {1}% for every {2} hero level(s).
Onkos
All the world’s a stage, and all the skeletons merely players.

Onkos spent his life as a theatre director, constantly travelling and clashing with actors over artistic vision. In frustration, he turned to puppets, but they lacked the grandeur of live performance. So why not use necromancy? The perfect actors were already waiting beneath the flesh.
To Onkos, the world is a stage, he is the director of its drama, and his beloved skeletons are both puppets and performers at once.
Perfect Puppets

Skeleton growth in your cities increases by {0}.
Under his command, Skeletons gain {1} Speed, {1} Initiative, and {2}% HP. Their Attack and Defense increase by {3} for every {4} hero levels, and enemy Skeletons lose an equal amount of Attack and Defense.
Kel’Ghul
He had a name once. A face. Now, he is only war.

Once known as Kelianor Ghulermo, Kel’Ghul defended his city against an invasion. Aid never came, and he fell alongside his men — only to rise again as undead warriors and drive back the invaders.
Bitter over the betrayal, Kel’Ghul cast aside his former life. Now, he roams with his undead army, seeking war wherever it may be found.
Dreadful War

Dread Knight growth in your cities increases by {0}.
Under his command, Dread Knights gain {1} Speed, {1} Initiative, and {2}% HP. Their Attack and Defense increase by {3} for every {4} hero levels, and enemy Dread Knights lose an equal amount of Attack and Defense.
Natalida
People scheme, betray, and backstab. Beasts have no hidden agendas — they’re the only ones you can trust.

Natalida became undead through a cruel twist of fate — a vampire lord fell in love with her and turned her against her will. She tried to escape, but his influence over her small town was too strong; even her own neighbors could not shelter her before they would break under his thrall and return her to him. Facing betrayal at every turn, she found refuge in the wilds, where beasts and abandoned pets became her only family.
Now a packmother, Natalida hunts alongside her ever‑loyal companions.
Part of a Pack

Undead Pet growth in your cities increases by {0}.
Under her command, Undead Pets gain {1} Speed, {1} Initiative, and {2}% HP. Their Attack and Defense increase by {3} for every {4} hero levels, and enemy Undead Pets lose an equal amount of Attack and Defense.
Artorius Veritas
Once a noble knight and monster hunter — now the very thing he swore to slay.

Artorius Veritas was once a renowned knight, a hero who slayed monsters and aided the weak. But when he fell to an ancient vampire, he became what he once hunted.
Though he escaped his sire, his fellow knights saw only a monster. Desperate, he turned to the Necromancers Guild, offering his service in exchange for the blood he needed to survive. He still fights, but his unrelenting fury poisons both friend and foe as he struggles to accept what he has become.
Drive ’em Crazy

He starts with the “Masterful Berserk” spell. This version of the spell affects all creatures adjacent to the target.
The cost of this spell is reduced by {0} mana for every {1} hero level(s).
Marl
The stillness of the earth… The only call worth heeding…

Marl is a simple zombie raised by Funerella to do physical work. However, the body he was raised from had remained in the soil for too long, a piece of the Murmurwood lodged within. As a result, there’s a bond between him and the earth, the latter gently listening to the him and guiding him like an echo — be it by turning into a swamp beneath his enemies or into a smooth path before his feet.
While not too smart and a bit irrational, he is a diligent and loyal servant of the tempestuous Funerella.
Languid Soil

He starts with the “Masterful Web” spell. This version of the spell affects all enemies.
While casting this spell, effective Spell Power is increased by {0} for every {1} hero level(s).
Tarius
Become part of the breathless tide!

Once among Jadame’s most powerful necromancers, Tarius was sealed away — until mortal greed freed him. Necromancy is more than raising the dead; it’s the reconstruction of body, soul… and patience. For now, he serves the Necromancers Guild, waiting. But when his full power returns, the world will remember why it once feared — and imprisoned — him.
Undead Legion
He has +{0}% Necromancy Power, plus another {1}% for every {2} hero level(s).
+{0} maximum Necromantic Energy, plus another +{1} for every {2} hero level(s).
Zam
An alchemist, first and foremost. A vampire? Distant second.

Zam was a reclusive alchemist, wholly consumed by his craft and untouched by the need for companionship. Even the love of a vampire maid, who once longed for his heart, failed to stir him. He turned her away with cold indifference and returned to his experiments.
Now in service to the Necromancers Guild, Zam toils away in a quiet lab, free from mortal needs and distractions. To him, undeath is no curse — it is the perfect solitude he always sought, sealed in a world of potions and unbroken silence.
Alchemist
He produces +{0} Mercury per day, plus another +{1} for every {2} hero level(s). Increases the amount of Mercury found on the map by {3}%.
Mag
To a true scholar of magic, the body is nothing but dead weight.

Mag was once a master of Arcane Magic, seeking to push the limits of space and time. A unique ritual granted him deep understanding — but at a cost. Now a stream of thought akin to a ghost, he has lost much of his power, his sense of self, and even his body, which now wanders the world on its own.
Though he can no longer live among the living, his mastery of magic surpasses all. The Necromancers Guild offering him a place to continue his studies is merely a bonus.
Arcane Dominion

He receives +{0} level to all Arcane spells, plus another +{1} for every {2} hero levels. Casting Arcane spells does not prevent him from using other spells of the same Magic School. Enemy hero cannot cast Arcane spells.
Adahn
He does not know himself. And so the torn and unsure flock to him, to find solace in unity, if not in memories that were shed.

Adahn awakened in Jadame with no memories of who he was. He might have been a victim of cruel experiments, a warrior who sacrificed his past, or just a necromancer’s pet zombie — he simply doesn’t know.
All that remains is a haunting void where his past should be, drawing restless ghosts to his forgotten existence.
Amnesiac

Wight growth in your cities increases by {0}.
Under his command, Wights gain {1} Speed, {1} Initiative, and {2}% HP. Their Attack and Defense increase by {3} for every {4} hero levels, and enemy Wights lose an equal amount of Attack and Defense.
Ethric
Truth lies buried deeper than bones — and it is twice as cursed.

Once a brilliant but reclusive wizard of Bracada, Ethric delved too deeply into death magic, stumbling upon secrets no mortal was meant to learn. In his attempt to conceal his findings, he became the target of a betrayal that nearly cost him his life. Ethric embraced undeath to survive, becoming a lich. Yet he still clings to the pretense of life, walking among mortals with a cryptic purpose. In the shadowed corners of the world, whispers of his fate grow louder, and he is now known to some as Ethric the Mad.
Tomb‑bound Will

Lich growth in your cities increases by {0}.
Under his command, Liches gain {1} Speed, {1} Initiative, and {2}% HP. Their Attack and Defense increase by {3} for every {4} hero levels, and enemy Liches lose an equal amount of Attack and Defense.
Guildmaster Klastor
The best in the realm at finding bones… and striking good deals with bad people.

Klastor knew there was no better way to profit than by being among necromancers. With a shovel, a bit of preparation, and the will to scour battlefields, he built a thriving business.
His knack for sensing the next grim harvest drew many gravediggers into his ranks — after they paid him his share. It’s lucrative work, but make no mistake — Klastor goes where the money is, and for now, the necromancers pay well for fresh corpses.
Bones Guild

Graverobber growth in your cities increases by {0}.
Under his command, Graverobbers gain {1} Speed, {1} Initiative, and {2}% HP. Their Attack and Defense increase by {3} for every {4} hero levels, and enemy Graverobbers lose an equal amount of Attack and Defense.
Shadespinner Oona
In darkness, all truths are laid bare — desires, souls, fears. She sees them. She weaves them.

As a child, Oona prayed to Naira to get a glimpse of her brother’s hidden soul. She succeeded — but at the cost of a streak of grey hair. Seeking more truths, she peered into her mother’s soul, then the village elder’s. Each revelation carried its price.
Over time, Oona mastered the art of unveiling secrets. Though each spell brings her closer to age and death, neither has claimed her yet.
Shadowmastery

She receives +{0} level to all Nightshade spells, plus another +{1} for every {2} hero levels. Casting Nightshade spells does not prevent her from using other spells of the same Magic School. Enemy hero cannot cast Nightshade spells.
Laura
Cursed. Hideous. Rotting. Spreading decay. Don’t look.

Some say beauty is a woman’s greatest asset — a belief Laura once held dear. Desperate to preserve her youth, she devised a spell to steal it from another woman. But the magic backfired.
Now cursed, her body endlessly decays without ever fully rotting, and all that she touches withers. She hides her face, hoping to stop dreaming of the youth she still covets. But sometimes, hope is the cruelest curse of all.
Moldering

She starts with the “Masterful Despair” spell. This version of the spell affects all types of enemies.
While casting this spell, effective Spell Power is increased by {0} for every {1} hero level(s).
Lord Rufus
How dare time move forward and leave him behind? He won’t ever let reality slip from his grasp again.

Long ago, Lord Rufus begged his beloved wife to embrace vampirism so they could remain together. She refused. Even resurrection spells failed, as if her very soul resisted him.
When she passed, the lord’s grief turned to anger as the world moved on without her. Now, Rufus ensures that those he fancies never leave — turning them into vampires against their will, Natalida among them. He will not make the same mistake twice. No one he “loves” will ever escape him again.
Wistful Nostalgia

He starts with the “Masterful Rewind Life” spell. This version of the spell can revive units beyond their original amount.
While casting this spell, effective Spell Power is increased by {0} for every {1} hero level(s).
Funerella
Putting the “fun” back in “funeral” — one grave at a time.

Born the eldest daughter of a baron, Funerella was spoiled and capricious. The slightest mistake from her servants would lead to whippings — or worse. One day, in a fit of temper, she killed a worker and raised him as a skeleton, declaring that the dead served better. Necromancy came so naturally to her that soon, the entire barony, including her father, became her obedient deathless servants.
Self‑absorbed, tempestuous, and utterly charming — she would see entire kingdoms bow before her.
Undead Legion
She has +{0}% Necromancy Power, plus another {1}% for every {2} hero level(s).
+{0} maximum Necromantic Energy, plus another +{1} for every {2} hero level(s).
Milossa the Golden
Her father, a golden swine. Her mother, a gilded moth. She alone walks on, weeping gold for their avarice.

Milossa was the daughter of Milos, a greedy duke obsessed with gold. After attempting to capture the legendary Mearea, he was cursed to absorb gold into his body, wracked with excruciating pain.
Choosing a holy path, Milossa took pity on her dying father and transferred the curse to herself — along with his sins — before leaving. Years later, she returned, undead and transformed, doomed to weep tears of gold until every last coin of her father’s wealth had been shed from her hollow eyes.
Wealthy
She produces +{0} Gold per day, plus another +{1} for every {2} hero level(s). Increases the amount of Gold found on the map by {3}%.
Nihil
She cannot undo the crime, but she can sever the guilt and fight for a cause that will save everyone.

Nihil, once a garrison guard, failed in her duty, allowing necromancers to launch a devastating surprise attack. As her comrades fell, she fled. Haunted by guilt, she began hearing the voice of the Dragonfly King, beckoning her to join the Chorus. Desperate to resist, she cut her hair and cast aside her name, becoming “Nothing.”
Now, she aids those who fight against demons, both within and without. Her crimes remain, but she hopes to earn redemption.
Wayfarer
She has +{0}% Movement points for every {1} hero level(s). Moves significantly more efficiently on roads.
Blackhorn
With frozen horns and burning wrath, he delves where others fear to tread.

After Alvar surfaced, underground excavations required special permission. Mardit defied this law, disgracing his clan, who were all forced to pay the price with their horns. His youngest daughter vanished, while his sons reacted in starkly different ways.
The elder, then known as Mardit son of Mardit, renounced both his father’s name and the traditions that bound him. Fueled by anger, he mastered ice magic, forging horns of Black Ice and pursuing the truth behind his father’s quest — bearing his new name: Blackhorn.
Spellweaver
He deals +{0}% Damage with spells, plus another {1}% for every {2} hero level(s). The Damage from the enemy hero’s spells is reduced by the same value.
Matastala the White
Politics is just a long game of chess. At least Gleard is a compelling opponent.

Matastala does not fully embrace the Abyss but seeks balance; she allies with the Schism not out of faith, but to be a steady voice among them.
Renowned for her intellect and logic, she serves as an advisor and engages in a perpetual game of chess with Gleard, their rivalry sharpening both minds. Amidst the Schism’s chaos, Matastala always finds a way to amuse herself.
Chessmaster
In the Tactics Phase, she has +{0} hex to the creature placement range. Creatures in her army gain {1} Initiative for every {2} hero level(s).
In the Tactics Phase, she has +{0} hex to the creature placement range and can see the positions of neutral stacks. Creatures in her army gain {1} Initiative for every {2} hero level(s).
Jänhei
In a world where everyone looks for power in the Rift, there is more power in staying independent from the Rift.

Jänhei fights to preserve his individuality against the Abyssal pull. He joined the Schism not only to battle the Hive but to shape a future free from the Rift’s grasp.
An adventurer and opportunist, he seeks kindred spirits among elves who refuse to yield, determined to forge his own path amid the chaos.
Roguish

Cultist growth in your cities increases by {0}.
Under his command, Cultists gain {1} Speed, {1} Initiative, and {2}% HP. Their Attack and Defense increase by {3} for every {4} hero levels, and enemy Cultists lose an equal amount of Attack and Defense.
Mara Mat’ha
“Wheröthmarash mathash thövish sosh.” We are siblings in cold blood, forever.

Mara Mat’ha was instrumental in forming the Schismatics’ Recon Cavalry during their first brutal northern winter. When traditional mounts failed, he devised a ritual to bond riders with Rift slugs — hardy beasts immune to the cold.
By splitting his mind and leaving part of it in the Abyss, he created a sacrament that merged his instincts with an aga’shoth. This transformation reshaped him, and so he took on a title that simply means “Blood Brother.”
Coldblood Siblings

Aga’Shoth Rider growth in your cities increases by {0}.
Under his command, Aga’Shoth Riders gain {1} Speed, {1} Initiative, and {2}% HP. Their Attack and Defense increase by {3} for every {4} hero levels, and enemy Aga’Shoth Riders lose an equal amount of Attack and Defense.
The Iron Master
Obedience and control are the only way to maintain true power.

The Iron Master remains an enigma. Whether mortal or something born of the Abyss, his true nature is unknown. Whatever he is, his presence exudes undeniable power, drawing the boldest of the Schismatics to his side.
His followers seek his guidance to resist the creeping corruption within their souls. Some whisper that he mesmerizes them, while others believe instinct binds them to him. In the end, they join him willingly.
Leash of Chains

Concubus growth in your cities increases by {0}.
Under his command, Concubi gain {1} Speed, {1} Initiative, and {2}% HP. Their Attack and Defense increase by {3} for every {4} hero levels, and enemy Concubi lose an equal amount of Attack and Defense.
Wal’kha
“We haven’t opened up the Rift not to have fun, have we?”
